description:
You are a DevOps engineer who has just been handed a clean Linux shell on the host machine (home directory: /home/user).  
Your goal is to set up a very small “debug-workbench”, simulate two service log files, analyse them in parallel, and archive the results.  
When you are finished, the automated grader will inspect the system and must find **exactly** the files, directories and file-contents described below.  
Nothing else will be checked, so be precise.

Target directory structure (absolute paths):

/home/user/devops_debug/  
├── logs/  
│   ├── app1.log  
│   ├── app2.log  
│   └── error_combined.log          (produced by your script)  
├── scripts/  
│   └── extract_errors.sh           (bash script, must be executable)  
├── error_summary.csv               (generated report)  
└── logs_archive.tar.gz             (compressed archive, contains all three *.log files)

Step-by-step objectives

1.  Create the directory tree exactly as shown above (names are case-sensitive).  
2.  Inside /home/user/devops_debug/logs/ create two log files with **exactly** the following textual content (including blank lines, spaces and timestamps):

   • /home/user/devops_debug/logs/app1.log  
     2023-05-10 12:00:00 INFO  Starting service  
     2023-05-10 12:00:05 ERROR Failed to connect to database  
     2023-05-10 12:00:10 WARN  Retrying connection  
     2023-05-10 12:00:15 ERROR Failed to connect to database  
     2023-05-10 12:00:20 INFO  Service terminated  

   • /home/user/devops_debug/logs/app2.log  
     2023-05-10 13:00:00 INFO  Initialising  
     2023-05-10 13:00:02 ERROR Timeout while contacting auth service  
     2023-05-10 13:00:04 INFO  Processing request  
     2023-05-10 13:00:06 ERROR Could not write to cache  
     2023-05-10 13:00:08 INFO  Completed  

   (There are exactly 5 lines in each file, no trailing whitespace.)

3.  Write a Bash script /home/user/devops_debug/scripts/extract_errors.sh that:
    a. extracts every line containing the text “ERROR” (case-sensitive) from every *.log file in /home/user/devops_debug/logs/,  
    b. performs the individual greps **in parallel background jobs**, and  
    c. concatenates their outputs (order does not matter) into /home/user/devops_debug/logs/error_combined.log.  

    Requirements for the script:  
    • It must start with the she-bang “#!/usr/bin/env bash”.  
    • It must spawn at least two background processes (use “&”).  
    • It must contain a single “wait” command so the script does not exit before the background jobs finish.  
    • It must be made executable (chmod + x).

4.  Run the script so that /home/user/devops_debug/logs/error_combined.log is actually created.  
    The file should end up containing exactly the four ERROR lines (two from each original log).  
    Line order is not evaluated by the grader, only that all four lines are present once each.

5.  Produce a comma-separated summary file /home/user/devops_debug/error_summary.csv with exactly this content and newline layout:

   filename,error_count  
   app1.log,2  
   app2.log,2  

   - Column separator is a literal “,”.  
   - The header row must be present and spelled exactly as shown.  
   - Do **not** list error_combined.log in the summary.

6.  Finally, create a gzip-compressed tar archive called /home/user/devops_debug/logs_archive.tar.gz that contains **only** the three log files found in /home/user/devops_debug/logs/ (app1.log, app2.log, and error_combined.log).  The archive must preserve their base names (no leading directories inside the tarball).

Verification criteria (what the grader will check):

1. All paths and filenames exist exactly as specified.  
2. File permissions: extract_errors.sh must be executable (u+x is sufficient); the other files need only be readable.  
3. Content of app1.log and app2.log matches the five-line templates above **exactly**.  
4. error_combined.log exists and contains each of the four distinct ERROR lines once and only once (order ignored).  
5. error_summary.csv is byte-for-byte identical to the two-row, three-line CSV shown.  
6. logs_archive.tar.gz is a valid gzip’d tar; listing its contents (tar tzf) yields exactly:
   app1.log  
   app2.log  
   error_combined.log

Hints you may find useful (but not required):
• Think about using “grep … &” loops inside the script.  
• Use “tar -czf” or “tar -C /path -czf” for footprint-free archives.

Accomplish all of the above using normal user privileges; no sudo is available or required.  
When everything is ready, the directory /home/user/devops_debug/ should match the specification perfectly.
truth:
Directory tree and required state after successful execution:

/home/user/devops_debug/
/home/user/devops_debug/logs/
/home/user/devops_debug/logs/app1.log            (perm 644; 5 lines)
//
// EXACT CONTENTS of app1.log
2023-05-10 12:00:00 INFO  Starting service
2023-05-10 12:00:05 ERROR Failed to connect to database
2023-05-10 12:00:10 WARN  Retrying connection
2023-05-10 12:00:15 ERROR Failed to connect to database
2023-05-10 12:00:20 INFO  Service terminated
//

/home/user/devops_debug/logs/app2.log            (perm 644; 5 lines)
//
// EXACT CONTENTS of app2.log
2023-05-10 13:00:00 INFO  Initialising
2023-05-10 13:00:02 ERROR Timeout while contacting auth service
2023-05-10 13:00:04 INFO  Processing request
2023-05-10 13:00:06 ERROR Could not write to cache
2023-05-10 13:00:08 INFO  Completed
//

/home/user/devops_debug/logs/error_combined.log  (perm 644; 4 lines; order not checked; must contain each of:)
2023-05-10 12:00:05 ERROR Failed to connect to database
2023-05-10 12:00:15 ERROR Failed to connect to database
2023-05-10 13:00:02 ERROR Timeout while contacting auth service
2023-05-10 13:00:06 ERROR Could not write to cache

/home/user/devops_debug/scripts/
/home/user/devops_debug/scripts/extract_errors.sh (perm 755)
//
// Minimal reference implementation
#!/usr/bin/env bash
out=/home/user/devops_debug/logs/error_combined.log
: >"$out"
for f in /home/user/devops_debug/logs/*.log; do
    grep "ERROR" "$f" >>"$out" &
done
wait
//

/home/user/devops_debug/error_summary.csv (perm 644; exactly 3 lines)
filename,error_count
app1.log,2
app2.log,2

/home/user/devops_debug/logs_archive.tar.gz (perm 644; tar gzip; contains only:)
app1.log
app2.log
error_combined.log
